name: Windsor Castle, Stonehenge & Bath Day Trip from London
tagline: Britain's oldest royal residence, the world's most famous prehistoric monument, and a Georgian spa city — all in one day
overview: The single most popular day trip from London combines three of England's greatest treasures: Windsor Castle — the oldest inhabited castle in the world and the King's weekend residence — with the prehistoric mystery of Stonehenge and the Roman-Georgian elegance of Bath. One of GetYourGuide's highest-reviewed European day trips with over 22,000 ratings.
highlights: Visit Windsor Castle, the world's oldest inhabited castle
Stand before Stonehenge with expert guide commentary
Free time to explore Roman Baths and Georgian Bath

itinerary.description: Explore the State Apartments, St George's Chapel (burial place of 10 monarchs including Henry VIII), and the magnificent Round Tower. The castle has been in continuous royal use for over 900 years.
Stand at one of humanity's greatest architectural achievements — 5,000 years old and still perfectly aligned with the summer solstice sunrise. Guided commentary brings the neolithic world to life.
Free time to explore the Roman Baths museum (entry not included), browse the famous Pulteney Bridge, or relax in one of the many Georgian tearooms.

meeting_point: London Victoria or Trafalgar Square area — confirmed at booking
tips: Arrive 10 minutes before departure — coaches leave on time
Windsor can be busy — the skip-the-line entry saves 45–60 minutes
Bath has excellent lunch options near the abbey — plan your stop there

faqs.question: Is Windsor Castle always open?
faqs.answer: Windsor occasionally closes for state events. Your guide will confirm access before departure. In rare cases, the exterior and grounds remain accessible.

faqs.question: Is Stonehenge entry included?
faqs.answer: Yes, Stonehenge timed entry is included in the tour price.

hero_image.alt: Windsor Castle Round Tower reflected in the Thames on a clear morning
